hooks.command: CMD=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',d).get('command',''))" 2>/dev/null || true); case "$CMD" in *grep*|*rg\ *|*ripgrep*|*find\ *|*fd\ *|*ack\ *|*ag\ *)   [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ] &&   echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"graphify: knowledge graph at graphify-out/. Only worth it for multi-file questions: if this search serves a cross-file or architecture question (tracing a flow, what-depends-on-X, anything spanning 3+ files), `graphify query \"<question>\"` returns one scoped subgraph instead of repeated searches. For single-symbol or known-file lookups, ignore this and proceed."}}'   || true ;; esac

HIT=$(python3 -c "import json,sys;d=json.load(sys.stdin);t=d.get('tool_input',d);s=(str(t.get('path') or '')+' '+str(t.get('glob') or '')).lower().replace(chr(92),'/');sys.stdout.write('' if 'graphify-out' in s else '1')" 2>/dev/null || true); if [ "$HIT" = 1 ] && [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ]; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"graphify: knowledge graph at graphify-out/. Only worth it for multi-file questions: if this search serves a cross-file or architecture question (tracing a flow, what-depends-on-X, anything spanning 3+ files), `graphify query \"<question>\"` returns one scoped subgraph instead of repeated searches. For single-symbol or known-file lookups, ignore this and proceed."}}'; fi || true
